$comment: c103 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH): runtime witness for the __umulh compiler intrinsic -- high 64 bits of the u64*u64 128-bit product. Lowers to a DEDICATED MIR UMulH op -> x86-64 `mul r/m64` (capture RDX, the 'high' role) / arm64 native `umulh`, NOT an import. Operands (a=0x123456789ABCDEF0, b=2^32) give high64=0x12345678 (nonzero AND != the low word 0x9ABCDEF000000000) -- a RAX/RDX role swap returns low & 0xFF = 0 != 120 and a missing lowering fails to compile (the design-audit's role-swap guard). The `release` arm (shippedPipeline, zero-drift) proves the intrinsic survives the full optimizer: Inlining folds umulh_high into main, and UMulH -- pure + commutative but NOT ConstFold-folded -- stays live. exit = high64 & 0xFF = 0x78 = 120.
